name: Rehoboam and Shishak
notes: Kings and Chronicles both recount Rehoboam's reign in Judah and Shishak king of Egypt's invasion in his fifth year. The shared core runs along the same beats: Rehoboam reigning in Jerusalem at forty-one for seventeen years with his mother Naamah an Ammonitess, Shishak coming up against Jerusalem, the carrying away of the treasures of the house of the LORD and the king's house and the gold shields Solomon made, Rehoboam replacing them with brasen shields kept by the guard, the rest of his acts and the war with Jeroboam, and his burial in the city of David with his son reigning after him. Kings tells it briefly and frames Judah's idolatry, high places, and sodomites (14:22-24), which Chronicles does not detail here. Chronicles tells it more fully, adding Shishak's forces, Shemaiah the prophet, and the princes and king humbling themselves so the LORD grants deliverance (12:3-8, 12) — matter unique to its vantage and left unlinked. The two are complementary vantages on one history, Kings compressing what Chronicles unfolds.
